spec_match.rationale: The page now combines the visible WHATWG JSON MIME classifier with a real browser JSON module import probe using the current Content-Type as a data: URL media type.

interactive.rationale: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148.0.0.0: presets and text input update the verdict, rule trace, and essence; the browser probe imports JSON modules for application/ld+json, image/svg+json, and Application/Problem+JSON with parameters.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: The UI covers literal application/json and text/json, broad */*+json suffixes, uppercase and parameterized Content-Type values, non-JSON fallbacks such as text/plain, and malformed values like not-a-mime.

references.rationale: The concept page now cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5470594816278528, matching the feature page and conformance route.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

summary: Repaired and re-reviewed with chrome-devtools-mcp. Before the fix, the page linked the wrong/nonexistent ChromeStatus feature 5085327190196224 and only demonstrated a hand-written classifier. After the fix, it links feature 5470594816278528, includes expanded presets, imports real JSON modules for application/ld+json, image/svg+json, and uppercase parameterized +JSON types, blocks text/plain and malformed not-a-mime without console errors, and the updated conformance route reports 5 pass / 0 fail.
